segments.role: 把系统需求转换为可制造芯片：工具链、IP block、验证、物理设计、tape-out 服务。
segments.products: EDA suites, verification, digital/analog IP, processor IP, design services, ASIC design enablement.
segments.value_pool: 高毛利、低资本开支、强续费属性；价值量来自设计复杂度、先进节点与IP复用。
segments.cycle: 相对抗周期，但新设计启动、先进节点迁移和并购整合会影响增速。
segments.competition: 全球高度集中：Synopsys/Cadence/Siemens EDA主导；Arm/Rambus/CEVA/eMemory/Alchip/GUC等分层竞争。
segments.kpis: RPO/ARR, design starts, emulation capacity, IP royalty, tape-out wins, advanced-node exposure.
segments.valuation: 偏软件/SaaS倍数，核心看续费粘性、AI/EDA加速、IP royalty runway和监管并购风险。
segments.questions: 先进节点设计成本是否继续上升？AI是否提高EDA ASP而非压缩设计服务？大客户自研ASIC会把IP价值池推向哪里？

segments.role: 承担数据中心、PC、边缘和嵌入式计算的核心逻辑芯片设计。
segments.products: GPU, CPU, AI accelerator, DPU, FPGA, custom ASIC, SoC.
segments.value_pool: AI训练/推理和平台生态带来最高利润池；先进封装、HBM和软件生态共同决定价值量。
segments.cycle: 需求由云资本开支、产品周期和供给约束驱动；库存周期弱于消费芯片但capex放缓会放大波动。
segments.competition: NVIDIA具生态优势；AMD/Broadcom/Marvell/ASIC suppliers追赶；Intel/Arm生态重新分配CPU价值。
segments.kpis: datacenter revenue, accelerator ASP, HBM attach, CoWoS capacity, backlog, cloud capex, software attach.
segments.valuation: 看AI revenue durability、gross margin ceiling、customer concentration和推理经济性。
segments.questions: AI capex是否产生足够ROI？custom ASIC会侵蚀GPU还是扩大市场？供应瓶颈从HBM/CoWoS转移到电力和网络了吗？
segments.downstream: Cloud, enterprise, OEM systems, industrial/auto edge

segments.role: 手机、IoT、CPE、汽车连接和消费电子的通信/射频/连接芯片。
segments.products: mobile SoC, modem, RF front-end, Wi-Fi/Bluetooth, GNSS, PMIC, display driver IC.
segments.value_pool: 价值量来自集成度、射频复杂度、客户平台粘性和高端机型mix。
segments.cycle: 手机换机、渠道库存和季节性强；RF受iPhone/Android旗舰周期影响明显。
segments.competition: Qualcomm/MediaTek/Apple internal modem、Skyworks/Qorvo/Maxscend/卓胜微等分层竞争。
segments.kpis: handset units, content per device, premium mix, design wins, channel inventory, China Android sell-through.
segments.valuation: 多按成熟半导体周期倍数；看库存拐点、客户集中度、Apple/China exposure。
segments.questions: 端侧AI是否提升SoC和memory BOM？Apple自研对外部RF/连接供应商影响多大？国产替代的ASP是否能守住？
segments.downstream: Smartphones, IoT, auto connectivity, consumer electronics

segments.name_en: switch ASIC, NIC, DPU, DSP, retimer
segments.role: 在AI集群里移动数据：交换、互联、SerDes、DSP、retimer、SmartNIC/DPU。
segments.products: Ethernet switch ASIC, routing silicon, optical DSP, retimer, CXL/PCIe, DPU/NIC.
segments.value_pool: AI cluster scale-out使网络芯片和高速I/O价值量上升；软件生态和系统验证是壁垒。
segments.cycle: 跟随AI集群建设、以太网/InfiniBand升级、800G/1.6T端口迁移。
segments.competition: Broadcom/Marvell/NVIDIA/Cisco占据核心；Credo/Astera/MACOM等抓高速连接增量。
segments.kpis: switch radix, port speed mix, retimer attach, optical DSP ASP, cloud capex, rack architecture.
segments.valuation: 看AI networking TAM、custom silicon pipeline、gross margin和供给瓶颈持续性。
segments.questions: Ethernet是否继续吃InfiniBand份额？scale-up互联会让价值转向封装/硅光吗？
segments.upstream: Foundry, advanced packaging, optical components, EDA/IP
segments.downstream: Cloud clusters, networking OEMs, optical modules

segments.name_en: analog, power, mixed signal, sensor IC
segments.role: 把真实世界的电源、信号、传感和控制连接到数字系统。
segments.products: power management, converters, amplifiers, isolation, sensors, drivers, SiC/GaN power devices.
segments.value_pool: 长生命周期、碎片化SKU、高毛利；汽车/工业认证带来粘性。
segments.cycle: 工业和汽车库存周期显著；AI服务器电源链条提供结构性增量。
segments.competition: TI/ADI/Infineon/ST/NXP/ON/MPS等；中国模拟公司在中低端和国产替代中扩张。
segments.kpis: book-to-bill, distributor inventory, auto backlog, industrial PMI, SiC utilization, power attach.
segments.valuation: 成熟龙头看through-cycle FCF和库存拐点；成长公司看design-in质量和毛利稳定性。
segments.questions: 工业去库存何时结束？AI电源价值量能否抵消传统工业疲弱？SiC价格战是否破坏回报？
segments.downstream: Auto, industrial, data center power, consumer, telecom

segments.role: 提供计算系统的短期和长期数据存储，HBM成为AI训练核心瓶颈。
segments.products: DRAM, NAND, HBM, enterprise SSD, controllers, NOR flash, specialty memory.
segments.value_pool: 最周期的半导体利润池之一；HBM和enterprise SSD提高结构性毛利。
segments.cycle: 价格、bit supply、库存和capex纪律决定盈利；AI供需可能改变传统周期斜率但不会消灭周期。
segments.kpis: DRAM/NAND ASP, bit growth, inventory days, capex, HBM share, qualification status, SSD contract backlog.
segments.valuation: 看mid-cycle earnings、replacement cost、HBM mix和资本纪律；不宜只年化高景气EPS。
segments.questions: HBM是否挤压commodity DRAM供给形成双重紧缺？NAND合同制能否平滑周期？
segments.downstream: AI servers, smartphones, PCs, enterprise storage, autos

segments.role: 把芯片设计制造成晶圆；先进逻辑、成熟制程、特色工艺分属不同竞争逻辑。
segments.products: advanced logic, mature-node foundry, RF/BCD/power processes, memory fabs, specialty processes.
segments.value_pool: 先进节点资本强度极高但议价力强；成熟制程更看稼动率和本地化需求。
segments.cycle: 稼动率、capex节奏、客户库存和地缘政策共同驱动。
segments.competition: TSMC主导先进逻辑；Samsung/Intel追赶；UMC/SMIC/GlobalFoundries/Hua Hong等在成熟制程竞争。
segments.kpis: utilization, wafer ASP, node mix, capex, depreciation, customer concentration, capacity by geography.
segments.valuation: 先进foundry看技术领先和ROI；成熟foundry看周期底部P/B、utilization recovery和政策支持。
segments.questions: 先进制程是否继续赢家通吃？成熟制程本地化是否带来结构性过剩？Intel Foundry商业模式能否成立？
segments.downstream: Fabless chips, IDMs, OSAT, system OEMs

segments.role: 为晶圆厂提供制造设备，是半导体资本开支的核心传导层。
segments.products: lithography, etch, deposition, implant, CMP, clean, thermal, track, plating.
segments.value_pool: 技术垄断和客户验证周期带来高壁垒；AI/HBM/先进逻辑推动WFE强度上升。
segments.cycle: 最直接受fab capex周期影响；订单、backlog和客户扩产计划领先收入。
segments.competition: ASML、AMAT、LAM、TEL、KLA等全球龙头；中国设备国产替代在刻蚀/薄膜/清洗/CMP扩张。
segments.kpis: WFE TAM, backlog, book-to-bill, China sales mix, service revenue, installed base, node transition.
segments.valuation: 看订单可见度、出口管制风险、服务占比和技术迭代带来的wallet share。
segments.questions: WFE是否进入AI驱动的新上行周期？中国国产替代是否改变全球份额？出口限制对订单质量影响多大？
segments.upstream: Precision components, vacuum, optics, motion control, materials

segments.role: 用检测、量测和测试保障良率；先进节点和先进封装让价值量提升。
segments.products: inspection, metrology, ATE, handlers, probe cards, test sockets, yield analytics.
segments.value_pool: 良率价值高、客户切换成本高；AI/HBM/advanced packaging提高测试复杂度。
segments.cycle: 跟WFE和封测capex相关，但高端ATE/探针卡受新产品ramp影响更直接。
segments.kpis: test time, probe card ASP, AI/HBM socket demand, utilization, new tester platform adoption.
segments.valuation: 看新平台周期、客户集中度、AI测试时间和服务/耗材收入弹性。
segments.questions: HBM测试瓶颈是否成为独立价值池？良率控制在2nm/advanced packaging中是否继续升值？
segments.downstream: Fabs, OSAT, memory, AI chip vendors

segments.name_en: silicon wafers, gases, photoresist, CMP, wet chemicals
segments.role: 提供晶圆制造所需耗材，是良率、成本和供应链安全的基础。
segments.products: silicon wafers, SOI, photoresist, CMP slurry/pads, specialty gases, wet chemicals, masks.
segments.value_pool: 耗材属性带来稳定性，但单价和份额受客户认证、良率贡献和地缘本地化影响。
segments.cycle: 随晶圆开工率和先进制程用量波动；价格通常慢于WFE但库存调整也明显。
segments.competition: 日本/美国/欧洲供应商强；中国材料从湿电子化学品、CMP、气体向高端突破。
segments.kpis: wafer shipments, fab utilization, qualification wins, China localization, specialty gas pricing, EUV material demand.
segments.valuation: 看through-cycle volume、客户认证壁垒、国产替代空间和原材料成本传导。
segments.questions: 先进节点材料复杂度是否提升ASP？本地化会带来份额提升还是价格压力？
segments.upstream: Chemicals, industrial gases, quartz, silicon, energy

segments.role: 把裸die封装成可用器件；先进封装成为AI系统性能瓶颈。
segments.products: wirebond, flip chip, fan-out, SiP, 2.5D/3D, HBM stack, test services.
segments.value_pool: 传统封测竞争激烈；CoWoS/2.5D/HBM/SiP提高资本强度和利润弹性。
segments.cycle: 跟终端出货、AI accelerator ramp和封装产能扩张相关。
segments.kpis: advanced packaging capacity, utilization, capex, AI customer mix, test hours, substrate availability.
segments.valuation: 看先进封装占比、客户质量、capex ROI和传统业务周期底部。
segments.questions: OSAT能否在foundry captive packaging之外拿到AI利润池？HBM stack和CoWoS瓶颈如何分配利润？
segments.upstream: Substrate, PCB, test equipment, wafers, materials

segments.name_en: ABF substrate, HDI PCB, high-speed boards
segments.role: 把芯片、封装和系统连接起来；AI服务器提升层数、材料和良率要求。
segments.products: ABF substrate, BT substrate, HDI, high-layer PCB, copper-clad laminate, connectors/cables.
segments.value_pool: 价值量随layer count、线宽线距、低损耗材料和良率难度上升。
segments.cycle: AI服务器/网络需求强，消费电子PCB更周期；ABF扩产周期长。
segments.competition: Ibiden/Unimicron/Shinko/Nanya/AT&S/欣兴等；中国PCB在服务器和通信链条提升份额。
segments.kpis: layer count, ABF capacity, AI server PCB ASP, material mix, yield, hyperscaler order visibility.
segments.valuation: 看AI产品mix、良率爬坡、扩产纪律和客户认证。
segments.questions: AI PCB是不是新的盈利池？ABF扩产会否重演过剩？低损耗材料谁最受益？
segments.upstream: Copper foil, glass fiber, resin, specialty chemicals
segments.downstream: Advanced packaging, servers, networking, optical modules

segments.name_en: optical modules, lasers, DSP, silicon photonics
segments.role: 把数据中心和通信网络里的高速电信号转成光信号并传输。
segments.products: 400G/800G/1.6T modules, lasers, transceivers, coherent optics, silicon photonics, optical engines.
segments.value_pool: AI cluster scale-out驱动800G/1.6T升级；毛利由DSP/laser/良率/客户认证决定。
segments.cycle: 云资本开支、端口速率迁移和telecom库存共同决定。
segments.competition: Coherent/Lumentum/Fabrinet/Ciena/MACOM/Marvell/Credo与中国光模块龙头分工竞争。
segments.kpis: port speed mix, module ASP erosion, hyperscaler awards, laser yields, coherent vs datacom split.
segments.valuation: 看AI订单可持续性、价格下行速度、客户集中和硅光替代路径。
segments.questions: 光模块ASP下行会多快？CPO/硅光会重塑供应链还是延长现有龙头优势？
segments.downstream: Cloud data centers, telecom, networking OEMs

segments.name_en: server ODM, EMS, networking and storage systems
segments.role: 把芯片、板卡、电源、散热和机柜集成为服务器、网络设备和电子系统。
segments.products: AI servers, racks, switches, storage systems, EMS, ODM design, electronics manufacturing.
segments.value_pool: 利润率较低但周转高；AI服务器让液冷、机柜集成和供应链管理价值上升。
segments.cycle: 受云capex、品牌OEM订单、库存和产品切换影响。
segments.kpis: AI server revenue, rack-scale attach, backlog, inventory, customer concentration, working capital.
segments.valuation: 看订单质量、毛利稀释/提升、现金转换和客户集中风险。
segments.questions: ODM能否留住AI rack-scale价值？毛利率是否因工程复杂度改善？云客户是否压价？

segments.role: 半导体和AI基础设施的外溢工业层：电力、散热、连接器、精密零部件和自动化。
segments.products: data center power, UPS, liquid cooling, connectors, thermal materials, vacuum parts, automation.
segments.value_pool: 受益于AI数据中心capex和晶圆厂本地化建设；利润池分散但可持续性较强。
segments.cycle: 更接近工业capex和数据中心建设周期；订单可见度高但项目节奏影响收入。
segments.kpis: data center backlog, liquid cooling attach, power density, fab project awards, book-to-bill.
segments.valuation: 看AI infra持续性、项目毛利、供应瓶颈和资本开支纪律。
segments.questions: 半导体价值是否从chip向power/cooling/connector外溢？数据中心电力瓶颈会怎样改变系统架构？
segments.downstream: Fabs, data centers, servers, network systems

segments.role: 为电子系统提供基础元器件、传感和连接，是半导体景气的低频传导层。
segments.products: MLCC, inductors, resistors, filters, sensors, connectors, modules.
segments.value_pool: 车规、工业、高频高速产品提升ASP；消费链条更价格竞争。
segments.cycle: 库存周期明显；汽车电子和AI服务器带来结构性高端mix。
segments.kpis: MLCC inventory, auto content, high-frequency product mix, distributor sell-through, utilization.
segments.valuation: 看周期底部、产品mix和客户分散度。
segments.questions: AI服务器和汽车能否抵消消费电子疲软？高端MLCC/电感国产替代会否压价？

eda_ip.submarkets: EDA core tools; verification/emulation; analog/custom IC design; physical implementation; IP licensing; ASIC design services; silicon lifecycle analytics.
eda_ip.model_drivers: Seat growth, token/cloud usage, maintenance renewal, IP royalties, hardware emulation backlog, design services NRE, China/export-control exposure.
eda_ip.leading_indicators: Advanced-node tape-outs, hyperscaler ASIC programs, foundry PDK releases, RPO/remaining performance obligations, emulation lead times, hiring in chip design teams.
eda_ip.red_flags: Large customer insourcing, regulatory delay around major M&A, China restrictions, EDA seat saturation, weaker design starts in handset/consumer.
eda_ip.first_coverage_tasks: Build peer table separating software-like EDA from IP royalty and design-service models; map each Core company to node complexity and AI/custom-silicon exposure.

fabless_compute.submarkets: GPU/accelerators; server CPU; client CPU/GPU; FPGA/embedded; custom ASIC; DPU/NIC; edge AI SoC.
fabless_compute.model_drivers: Datacenter revenue, accelerator ASP, HBM/CoWoS allocation, software/networking attach, cloud capex, product cadence, gross margin by platform.
fabless_compute.leading_indicators: Hyperscaler capex revisions, AI server lead times, HBM supply contracts, TSMC advanced-node mix, ODM backlog, accelerator benchmark adoption.
fabless_compute.red_flags: Customer ROI fatigue, over-ordering, custom ASIC substitution, supply bottleneck moving outside chips, export restrictions, margin dilution from systems.
fabless_compute.first_coverage_tasks: Create AI compute scorecard: accelerator revenue, supply chain bottleneck, customer concentration, inference exposure, ecosystem lock-in and valuation sensitivity.

mobile_rf_connectivity.submarkets: Mobile AP/modem; RF front-end; Wi-Fi/Bluetooth; display driver; image sensor; IoT connectivity; touch/fingerprint/audio chips.
mobile_rf_connectivity.model_drivers: Handset units, premium mix, content per device, Android channel inventory, Apple content share, auto/IoT diversification, RF module integration.
mobile_rf_connectivity.leading_indicators: Smartphone sell-through, Taiwan fabless monthly revenue, China Android launch calendar, Apple build plans, distributor inventory, panel shipments.
mobile_rf_connectivity.red_flags: Apple insourcing, Android ASP pressure, inventory rebuild without sell-through, RF overcapacity, display driver commoditization.
mobile_rf_connectivity.first_coverage_tasks: Split companies into handset-beta, connectivity-beta, display-beta and image-sensor-beta; do not compare RF, CIS and DDI on one multiple without cycle adjustment.

networking_silicon.submarkets: Switch ASIC; routing silicon; optical DSP; retimer; SerDes IP; DPU/NIC; active electrical cable; CXL/PCIe fabric.
networking_silicon.model_drivers: Port-speed transition, switch radix, retimer attach rate, optical DSP ASP, custom silicon wins, cloud capex and AI cluster topology.
networking_silicon.leading_indicators: 800G/1.6T awards, Ethernet vs InfiniBand adoption, hyperscaler network architecture, Arista/Cisco order commentary, optical-module lead times.
networking_silicon.red_flags: ASP erosion faster than port upgrade, cloud customer concentration, internal silicon displacement, CPO timing uncertainty, one-time pull-ins.
networking_silicon.first_coverage_tasks: Map every company to scale-out, scale-up, electrical or optical layer; build a port-speed migration table from 400G to 800G to 1.6T.

analog_power.submarkets: Signal chain; power management; power discrete/modules; SiC/GaN; MCU; sensors; isolation; automotive radar/driver ICs.
analog_power.model_drivers: Industrial/auto inventory, channel sell-through, book-to-bill, utilization, pricing, SiC capacity, AI server power content.
analog_power.leading_indicators: Distributor inventory days, auto production schedules, industrial PMI, EV inverter demand, data-center power density, lead times.
analog_power.red_flags: Inventory correction masked by backlog, SiC price collapse, auto platform delays, China analog price competition, underloaded internal fabs.
analog_power.first_coverage_tasks: Separate long-cycle auto/industrial quality names from consumer analog and China localization names; normalize margins for utilization.

memory_storage.submarkets: Commodity DRAM; HBM; NAND; enterprise SSD; HDD/nearline storage; NOR/specialty memory; controllers/modules.
memory_storage.model_drivers: DRAM/NAND ASP, bit shipments, capex, inventory days, HBM qualification, enterprise SSD contract mix, storage nearline demand.
memory_storage.leading_indicators: Spot/contract memory pricing, memory capex plans, HBM supply agreements, hyperscaler storage orders, China smartphone/PC demand, module channel inventory.
memory_storage.red_flags: Annualizing peak ASP, HBM qualification loss, NAND overbuild, customer prebuy, capex discipline breakdown, hidden inventory at module makers.
memory_storage.first_coverage_tasks: Build memory cycle sheet: ASP, bit growth, utilization, capex and HBM mix; value companies on normalized earnings and scenario margins.

foundry_idm.submarkets: Leading-edge logic; mature foundry; specialty RF/BCD/power; IDM logic; specialty MEMS/sensor; captive advanced packaging.
foundry_idm.model_drivers: Utilization, wafer ASP, node mix, depreciation, capex, customer concentration, geographic capacity, subsidies, advanced packaging attach.
foundry_idm.leading_indicators: Foundry monthly revenue, smartphone/PC sell-through, cloud ASIC tape-outs, WFE orders, fab utilization, node migration schedules.
foundry_idm.red_flags: Mature-node overcapacity, depreciation cliff, technology slip, customer concentration, geopolitical disruption, underpriced government-led expansion.
foundry_idm.first_coverage_tasks: Separate advanced-node scarcity from mature-node cyclical recovery; build utilization and depreciation sensitivity by node and geography.

wfe.submarkets: Lithography; deposition; etch; clean; CMP; implant; thermal; track; compound semi tools; back-end equipment overlap.
wfe.model_drivers: WFE TAM, orders, backlog, China mix, service revenue, installed base, node transition intensity, memory vs logic capex.
wfe.leading_indicators: TSMC/Samsung/Intel/Micron/SK capex, SEMI/WFE forecasts, equipment lead times, export-control updates, service growth.
wfe.red_flags: China pull-forward, order cancellations, memory capex pause, customer concentration, export restrictions, domestic substitution at lower margin.
wfe.first_coverage_tasks: Build WFE wallet-share map by process step and customer; do not treat ASML/Lam/KLA/AMAT/TEL as interchangeable equipment beta.

process_control_test.submarkets: Metrology; inspection; ATE; probe cards; sockets/contactors; handlers; burn-in; system-level test; yield analytics.
process_control_test.model_drivers: Test time, probe card ASP, advanced-node process-control intensity, HBM/AI complexity, new tester platforms, consumables refresh.
process_control_test.leading_indicators: AI/HBM product ramps, KLA/Advantest/Teradyne orders, OSAT capex, yield bottlenecks, new package qualification.
process_control_test.red_flags: One-platform demand spike, customer concentration, memory test pull-in, probe card pricing pressure, lower utilization at OSATs.
process_control_test.first_coverage_tasks: Split process-control capital equipment from recurring probe/socket consumables; model AI/HBM test time as a separate driver.

materials_wafers.submarkets: Silicon wafers; SOI/engineered substrates; SiC/GaN substrates; photoresist; CMP slurry/pads; gases; wet chemicals; photomasks; carriers/pods.
materials_wafers.model_drivers: Fab utilization, wafer starts, qualification wins, advanced-node material intensity, LTAs, gas onsite contracts, raw-material costs.
materials_wafers.leading_indicators: Wafer shipments, fab utilization, WFE installation, foundry/memory capex, specialty gas pricing, EUV/advanced packaging material demand.
materials_wafers.red_flags: Inventory at fabs, price resets after LTAs, qualification delays, China localization price pressure, raw material cost squeeze.
materials_wafers.first_coverage_tasks: Create materials map by consumable frequency and qualification barrier; separate volume cyclicality from structural content growth.

osat_packaging.submarkets: Wirebond; flip chip; WLCSP; fan-out; SiP; 2.5D/3D; HBM stacking; test services; packaging equipment.
osat_packaging.model_drivers: Advanced packaging capacity, utilization, capex, AI customer mix, substrate availability, test hours, package ASP.
osat_packaging.leading_indicators: CoWoS/HBM capacity expansions, OSAT monthly revenue, substrate lead times, AI accelerator ramps, packaging equipment orders.
osat_packaging.red_flags: Traditional OSAT price pressure, captive foundry packaging share, capex without customer prepayment, utilization dip after ramp.
osat_packaging.first_coverage_tasks: Separate traditional OSAT from advanced-packaging equipment and AI packaging capacity; watch capital intensity and customer concentration.

substrate_pcb.submarkets: ABF substrate; BT substrate; HDI; high-layer server PCB; low-loss CCL/prepreg; connectors/cables; thermal PCB materials.
substrate_pcb.model_drivers: Layer count, ABF capacity, low-loss material mix, AI server/rack demand, yield, customer qualification, capex discipline.
substrate_pcb.leading_indicators: AI server ODM orders, GPU/ASIC substrate allocation, CCL pricing, Taiwan PCB monthly revenue, cloud switch port-speed transition.
substrate_pcb.red_flags: ABF overbuild, yield miss on high-layer boards, customer concentration, material shortages, server pull-forward.
substrate_pcb.first_coverage_tasks: Build AI server BOM bridge: substrate, PCB, CCL, connector/cable and thermal content per rack.

optical_photonics.submarkets: Datacom transceivers; telecom coherent optics; lasers; optical DSP; passive optical components; silicon photonics; CPO/optical engines.
optical_photonics.model_drivers: Port-speed mix, module ASP erosion, hyperscaler awards, laser yield, DSP content, 800G/1.6T ramp, telecom inventory.
optical_photonics.leading_indicators: Cloud optical awards, 800G/1.6T shipments, Arista/NVIDIA/Broadcom architecture, laser lead times, China optical monthly revenue.
optical_photonics.red_flags: Rapid ASP erosion, customer concentration, module commoditization, CPO timing uncertainty, telecom downturn spillover.
optical_photonics.first_coverage_tasks: Separate module assemblers from laser/DSP/component suppliers; build port-speed migration and ASP erosion scenarios.

ems_odm_systems.submarkets: AI server ODM; rack integration; networking white box; EMS; enterprise server OEM; storage systems; liquid-cooled system integration.
ems_odm_systems.model_drivers: AI server revenue, rack-scale attach, backlog, inventory, customer concentration, working capital, gross margin by system complexity.
ems_odm_systems.leading_indicators: Hyperscaler capex, ODM monthly revenue, GPU allocation, power/cooling availability, rack-scale design wins, networking switch demand.
ems_odm_systems.red_flags: Low-margin pass-through revenue, inventory financing, customer concentration, delayed GPU allocation, governance/accounting risk.
ems_odm_systems.first_coverage_tasks: Track AI server dollars and margin separately; compare ODMs on cash conversion, customer mix and rack-scale engineering content.

adjacent_infra.submarkets: Data-center power; UPS; cooling/liquid cooling; connectors; cables; vacuum components; precision machining; fab engineering; automation.
adjacent_infra.model_drivers: Backlog, project timing, power density, liquid-cooling attach, fab construction, WFE installed base, connector/cable content per rack.
adjacent_infra.leading_indicators: Data-center power awards, utility/grid constraints, liquid cooling adoption, fab build permits, WFE subsystem orders, ODM rack shipments.
adjacent_infra.red_flags: Project slippage, margin dilution from turnkey work, component commoditization, capex double-ordering, customer concentration.
adjacent_infra.first_coverage_tasks: Create an AI infrastructure adjacency screen; distinguish chip-cycle exposure from data-center construction and fab-services exposure.

passives_components.submarkets: MLCC; inductors; resistors; filters; ceramic packages; sensors; display components; modules.
passives_components.model_drivers: Distributor inventory, auto/industrial content, high-end MLCC mix, smartphone/PC units, server content, utilization.
passives_components.leading_indicators: MLCC lead times, auto production, smartphone builds, distributor sell-through, high-frequency component demand, Taiwan/Japan component monthly data.
passives_components.red_flags: Inventory rebound without end demand, low-end price competition, overcapacity, weak consumer electronics, auto destocking.
passives_components.first_coverage_tasks: Separate passive-cycle leaders from display-cycle names and specialty ceramic/package suppliers; normalize for inventory swings.

eda_ip.revenue_model: Subscription / maintenance / IP royalty / design-service NRE
eda_ip.margin_profile: Very high software-like gross margin for EDA/IP; lower but still attractive for ASIC services
eda_ip.cycle_beta: Low to medium; design activity is steadier than wafer shipments
eda_ip.competition: Highly concentrated global oligopoly in EDA; more fragmented in design services
eda_ip.ai_leverage: High through custom ASIC, accelerated design complexity and advanced-node verification
eda_ip.valuation_anchor: Recurring revenue growth, RPO, FCF margin, IP royalty durability

fabless_compute.revenue_model: Product sales, platform attach, networking/system bundles, custom silicon NRE and production revenue
fabless_compute.margin_profile: High for differentiated accelerators/platform chips; can dilute when systems pass-through rises
fabless_compute.capital_intensity: Low internal capex but high external supply-chain prepayments and inventory commitments
fabless_compute.cycle_beta: High, but currently dominated by cloud AI capex and supply constraints
fabless_compute.competition: Winner-take-most ecosystems, plus custom ASIC challengers and CPU incumbents
fabless_compute.valuation_anchor: AI revenue durability, gross margin ceiling, customer concentration, supply bottleneck control

mobile_rf_connectivity.revenue_model: Chip/module sales into handset, IoT, auto connectivity and consumer platforms
mobile_rf_connectivity.margin_profile: Medium to high; premium RF/CIS/SoC can be attractive, DDI and low-end connectivity more commoditized
mobile_rf_connectivity.capital_intensity: Low to medium; mostly fabless but inventory and module content matter
mobile_rf_connectivity.cycle_beta: High for handset/display, medium for IoT/connectivity
mobile_rf_connectivity.competition: Mix of global leaders, Apple/internal silicon risk and China localization challengers
mobile_rf_connectivity.ai_leverage: Medium through edge AI and premium device content
mobile_rf_connectivity.valuation_anchor: Unit cycle, content per device, customer concentration and inventory normalization

networking_silicon.revenue_model: Silicon product sales, custom silicon programs, optical DSP/retimer content and platform software attach
networking_silicon.margin_profile: High when IP and product differentiation are strong; module-adjacent silicon faces ASP pressure
networking_silicon.capital_intensity: Low internal capex; high dependence on advanced foundry and package allocation
networking_silicon.cycle_beta: High, tied to cloud AI capex and port-speed transitions
networking_silicon.competition: Broadcom/NVIDIA/Marvell plus high-growth specialists in retimer, SerDes and optical DSP
networking_silicon.valuation_anchor: Port-speed migration, attach rates, hyperscaler awards, custom silicon backlog

analog_power.revenue_model: Broad SKU chip/device/module sales, often through direct and distributor channels
analog_power.margin_profile: Medium to high; strongest in proprietary analog, auto/industrial and high-performance power
analog_power.capital_intensity: Medium to high for IDMs and SiC; low to medium for fabless analog
analog_power.cycle_beta: Medium to high; inventory cycles can be long and painful
analog_power.competition: Global incumbents with long-tail SKU depth; China challengers increasingly relevant
analog_power.ai_leverage: Medium, rising through AI server power and power density
analog_power.valuation_anchor: Through-cycle FCF, inventory inflection, design-in quality and utilization recovery

memory_storage.revenue_model: Commodity and specialty memory bit shipments, contract pricing, SSD/HDD/storage product sales
memory_storage.margin_profile: Highly cyclical; HBM and enterprise storage improve mix but do not eliminate cycle risk
memory_storage.competition: DRAM concentrated; NAND more competitive; HDD duopoly/oligopoly dynamics
memory_storage.ai_leverage: Very high through HBM and AI storage
memory_storage.valuation_anchor: Normalized earnings, memory ASP cycle, HBM mix, capex discipline

foundry_idm.revenue_model: Wafer sales, process platform services, specialty foundry, captive IDM product economics
foundry_idm.margin_profile: High at leading edge when scarce; lower and more volatile at mature nodes when utilization falls
foundry_idm.cycle_beta: High; utilization and depreciation drive earnings torque
foundry_idm.competition: TSMC dominates leading edge; mature nodes more regional and price competitive
foundry_idm.ai_leverage: High for advanced foundry and packaging; medium for mature nodes
foundry_idm.valuation_anchor: Utilization, node mix, capex ROI, depreciation, geopolitical risk premium

wfe.revenue_model: Tool sales, service, spares, upgrades and installed-base support
wfe.margin_profile: High for technology leaders and service-rich models
wfe.capital_intensity: Medium; R&D and supply chain are critical, but customers carry fab capex
wfe.cycle_beta: High; orders and backlog move before revenue
wfe.competition: Process-step oligopolies with high switching cost and long qualification cycles
wfe.ai_leverage: High through HBM, advanced logic and packaging intensity
wfe.valuation_anchor: WFE TAM, backlog quality, service mix, China exposure, process-step wallet share

process_control_test.revenue_model: Capital equipment, probe cards, sockets, handlers, consumables and services
process_control_test.margin_profile: High in process control and probe cards; medium in handlers and services
process_control_test.cycle_beta: Medium to high; less purely cyclical when consumables/service mix is high
process_control_test.competition: Strong niche leaders; advanced-node/test complexity raises barriers
process_control_test.ai_leverage: High through HBM, AI SoC test time and advanced packaging yield control
process_control_test.valuation_anchor: Test intensity, probe card ASP, platform cycles, installed base and recurring consumables

materials_wafers.revenue_model: Consumables, wafers, specialty gases, chemicals, photomasks, engineered substrates, LTAs
materials_wafers.margin_profile: Medium to high; best where qualification barriers and specialty content are high
materials_wafers.capital_intensity: Medium to high for wafers/gases; medium for specialty chemicals
materials_wafers.cycle_beta: Medium; tied to wafer starts and fab utilization
materials_wafers.competition: Japan/US/Europe strong in high-end materials; China localization expanding
materials_wafers.ai_leverage: Medium to high through advanced nodes, EUV, HBM and fab buildout
materials_wafers.valuation_anchor: Wafer starts, qualification wins, material intensity, pricing under LTAs

osat_packaging.revenue_model: Assembly, packaging, test services, advanced package capacity and packaging equipment
osat_packaging.margin_profile: Low to medium in traditional OSAT; higher in scarce advanced packaging equipment/capacity
osat_packaging.cycle_beta: High for traditional OSAT; high but supply-constrained for advanced packaging
osat_packaging.competition: OSAT scale leaders plus foundry captive advanced packaging
osat_packaging.ai_leverage: Very high through CoWoS, HBM and 2.5D/3D packaging
osat_packaging.valuation_anchor: Advanced packaging mix, utilization, capex ROI, customer pre-commitments

substrate_pcb.revenue_model: Substrate/PCB/CCL sales; product mix and layer count drive ASP
substrate_pcb.margin_profile: Medium; high-end ABF/AI PCB and low-loss materials can be stronger
substrate_pcb.cycle_beta: High; consumer PCB and substrate cycles differ from AI server demand
substrate_pcb.competition: Asian manufacturing leaders; customer qualification and yield matter
substrate_pcb.ai_leverage: Very high through AI server/rack substrate, PCB and low-loss material content
substrate_pcb.valuation_anchor: Layer count, ABF capacity, low-loss material mix, AI server share, yield

optical_photonics.revenue_model: Modules, lasers, optical components, optical DSP, manufacturing services and systems
optical_photonics.margin_profile: Medium; components/DSP can be higher, modules face ASP erosion
optical_photonics.cycle_beta: High; cloud awards and telecom cycles can diverge sharply
optical_photonics.competition: China module leaders, US component/laser/DSP names and contract manufacturing specialists
optical_photonics.ai_leverage: Very high through 800G/1.6T and cluster scale-out
optical_photonics.valuation_anchor: Port-speed migration, ASP erosion, customer awards, laser yield and product mix

ems_odm_systems.revenue_model: Manufacturing services, ODM design, rack/system integration, pass-through hardware revenue
ems_odm_systems.margin_profile: Low to medium; can improve with engineering complexity and rack-scale integration
ems_odm_systems.capital_intensity: Low to medium but working capital can be heavy
ems_odm_systems.cycle_beta: High; customer program timing and inventory swings matter
ems_odm_systems.competition: Scale manufacturing, supply-chain execution and customer trust
ems_odm_systems.ai_leverage: High through AI server and rack integration
ems_odm_systems.valuation_anchor: Cash conversion, AI server mix, customer concentration, gross margin sustainability

adjacent_infra.revenue_model: Power/cooling equipment, connectors/cables, fab services, precision subsystems, project revenue
adjacent_infra.margin_profile: Medium to high depending on product vs turnkey/project mix
adjacent_infra.cycle_beta: Medium; more tied to construction/project cycles than chip ASPs
adjacent_infra.competition: Industrial leaders plus high-spec component specialists
adjacent_infra.ai_leverage: High through power density, liquid cooling and rack/fab infrastructure
adjacent_infra.valuation_anchor: Backlog, power density, liquid cooling attach, project margin, component scarcity

passives_components.revenue_model: Electronic component sales through direct/distributor channels; mix drives ASP
passives_components.margin_profile: Medium; high-end auto/server components stronger than consumer commodity parts
passives_components.cycle_beta: Medium to high; distributor inventory cycles are important
passives_components.competition: Japan/Taiwan/Korea leaders with scale and material know-how
passives_components.ai_leverage: Medium through AI server power and high-frequency components
passives_components.valuation_anchor: Inventory cycle, high-end mix, utilization, auto/industrial content

eda_ip.cycle_type: 低-中 beta，偏长周期续费/设计活动，不跟晶圆出货一比一同步。
eda_ip.drivers: 先进节点设计复杂度、AI/ASIC tape-out、IP 复用率、客户研发预算和并购整合。
eda_ip.leading: RPO/ARR、设计启动数、emulation capacity、foundry PDK 节点节奏、IP royalty pipeline。
eda_ip.lagging: license revenue timing、IP royalty 实现、design-service 产能利用率和客户项目转量产。
eda_ip.margin: 软件续费和IP授权保护毛利；设计服务/NRE占比高时收入更项目化、毛利更波动。
eda_ip.valuation: 更接近软件/高质量IP估值；看 recurring growth、FCF margin、RPO 可见度，而不是简单套半导体周期倍数。
eda_ip.watchout: 单季 license 提前/推后、并购摊销或大客户 tape-out timing，容易被误读成需求拐点。

fabless_compute.cycle_type: 高 beta，但当前更多由云AI capex、供给瓶颈和产品代际共同驱动。
fabless_compute.drivers: hyperscaler capex、训练/推理ROI、GPU/ASIC产品周期、HBM/CoWoS供给、客户集中度。
fabless_compute.leading: 云厂商capex revision、accelerator backlog、HBM allocation、CoWoS lead time、ASIC tape-out/award。
fabless_compute.lagging: data center revenue、gross margin、inventory purchase commitments、软件/系统attach兑现。
fabless_compute.margin: 供不应求阶段毛利上行；系统/板卡pass-through、客户议价或推理价格下降会压缩毛利。
fabless_compute.valuation: 核心是AI revenue durability和增长持续期；不能只年化缺货期收入，也不能只用传统PC/CPU周期框架。
fabless_compute.watchout: 双重下单、客户ROI疲劳、供应瓶颈迁移到电力/网络、custom ASIC替代节奏。

mobile_rf_connectivity.cycle_type: 高消费电子 beta，单位周期、渠道库存和新品季节性高度重要。
mobile_rf_connectivity.drivers: 手机出货、Apple/Android build plan、高端机mix、RF/CIS/DDI/SoC内容量和渠道库存。
mobile_rf_connectivity.leading: China Android sell-through、Apple供应链订单、渠道库存、panel/handset数据、RF design win。
mobile_rf_connectivity.lagging: 芯片出货、ASP、utilization、毛利率和应收/库存周转。
mobile_rf_connectivity.margin: 库存去化末期毛利承压；新品拉货和高端mix改善时ASP/毛利恢复，但低端竞争会抵消。
mobile_rf_connectivity.valuation: 看库存底部后的normalized EPS和内容量提升，而不是把短期restock误认为新成长曲线。
mobile_rf_connectivity.watchout: sell-in强但sell-through弱、Apple自研、Android价格战、DDI/低端连接芯片商品化。

networking_silicon.cycle_type: 高 beta，和云AI网络建设、端口速率迁移、客户award节奏绑定。
networking_silicon.drivers: AI cluster topology、Ethernet vs InfiniBand、800G/1.6T迁移、retimer/DSP attach、custom silicon pipeline。
networking_silicon.leading: switch roadmap、hyperscaler award、SerDes IP采用、retimer attach、optical DSP design win。
networking_silicon.margin: 高性能IP可保毛利；模块邻近 silicon 若随光模块ASP erosion走，利润弹性会下降。
networking_silicon.valuation: 按端口迁移和客户award duration估值；需要把一次性pull-in和可持续平台份额分开。
networking_silicon.watchout: 客户自研、标准切换、CPO时间点、单一云客户订单集中。

analog_power.cycle_type: 中-高 beta，长设计周期叠加分销库存周期，是最容易被库存误读的环节之一。
analog_power.drivers: 工业PMI、汽车产量/EV内容量、分销商库存、book-to-bill、SiC/GaN价格和AI server power。
analog_power.leading: book-to-bill、distributor inventory、order cancellation、lead time、auto backlog、SiC utilization。
analog_power.lagging: revenue growth、fab utilization、gross margin、库存跌价和渠道补库。
analog_power.margin: utilization是毛利核心；收入小幅下滑也可能因固定成本吸收不足造成利润大幅波动。
analog_power.valuation: 成熟龙头看through-cycle FCF和库存拐点；成长公司看design-in质量、国产替代价格和SiC ROIC。
analog_power.watchout: 把渠道补库当成终端复苏、忽略utilization弹性、忽略中国模拟/功率价格竞争。

memory_storage.cycle_type: 非常高 beta，价格/库存/供给纪律驱动；HBM改善结构但不消灭周期。
memory_storage.drivers: DRAM/NAND ASP、bit supply、库存天数、capex纪律、HBM qualification和enterprise SSD demand。
memory_storage.leading: contract ASP、spot price、inventory days、capex cut/add、HBM share allocation、SSD订单。
memory_storage.lagging: gross margin、operating profit、cash flow、equipment orders和供应商议价。
memory_storage.margin: 价格弹性极高；HBM mix提升毛利，但commodity DRAM/NAND若过供仍会拖累整体利润。
memory_storage.valuation: 用mid-cycle earnings、replacement cost和HBM mix做情景；不要简单年化上行期EPS。
memory_storage.watchout: capex过度反应、隐藏渠道库存、HBM share loss、NAND价格短期反弹后的再过供。

foundry_idm.cycle_type: 高 beta，advanced node有结构性稀缺，mature node更像稼动率/价格周期。
foundry_idm.drivers: 客户tape-out、wafer starts、node mix、capex、depreciation、地缘/本地化政策。
foundry_idm.leading: 月度收入、utilization commentary、customer inventory、capex guidance、wafer ASP和node allocation。
foundry_idm.margin: 稼动率和node mix决定利润；折旧上行期若需求放缓，margin压缩会非常快。
foundry_idm.valuation: advanced foundry看技术领先和客户黏性；mature foundry看P/B、utilization recovery和政策支持质量。
foundry_idm.watchout: 把mature-node本地化误认为高ROIC成长、忽略折旧周期和客户库存调整。

wfe.cycle_type: 高 beta，最直接反映fab capex；订单/ backlog通常领先收入。
wfe.drivers: WFE TAM、foundry/memory capex、先进节点强度、China localization、export controls和service mix。
wfe.leading: orders、book-to-bill、backlog、China mix、客户capex计划、tool lead time。
wfe.lagging: tool revenue、service revenue、gross margin、供应链交付和客户验收。
wfe.margin: 规模和service mix保护利润；周期下行时订单先掉，收入和毛利滞后体现。
wfe.valuation: 看backlog质量、process-step wallet share、service recurring和出口限制风险，通常要用跨周期订单框架。
wfe.watchout: China pull-forward、出口限制造成的订单质量变化、把短期backlog当成永久增长。

process_control_test.cycle_type: 中-高 beta，但比WFE更受新产品ramp、良率难度和测试时间驱动。
process_control_test.drivers: advanced-node良率、HBM/AI SoC测试时间、probe card ASP、ATE平台更新、OSAT/memory capex。
process_control_test.leading: tester backlog、probe card订单、test time、yield bottleneck、new platform adoption。
process_control_test.margin: 高端probe/inspection毛利强；低端handler或项目型设备更周期。
process_control_test.valuation: 看测试强度和耗材/服务可重复性，而不是只跟随WFE总量。
process_control_test.watchout: ATE/probe周期与WFE不同步；客户平台切换可能造成短期订单真空。

materials_wafers.cycle_type: 中 beta，随wafer starts和utilization波动，价格/库存调整慢于设备。
materials_wafers.drivers: wafer starts、材料用量强度、先进节点/EUV/HBM需求、客户qualification和本地化。
materials_wafers.leading: wafer shipment、fab utilization、qualification win、specialty gas/photoresist pricing、客户库存。
materials_wafers.lagging: 材料收入、ASP、gross margin、长期供货协议兑现。
materials_wafers.margin: 认证壁垒和特殊材料保护毛利；原材料/能源成本和客户议价会滞后传导。
materials_wafers.valuation: 用through-cycle volume、份额提升和材料强度估值；国产替代需要区分份额和价格。
materials_wafers.watchout: 设备订单强不等于材料马上强；成熟制程过剩会压低用量和价格。

osat_packaging.cycle_type: 传统OSAT高 beta；先进封装也是高 beta，但常被供给瓶颈和客户预付款约束。
osat_packaging.drivers: 终端出货、AI accelerator ramp、CoWoS/2.5D/HBM capacity、OSAT utilization和substrate availability。
osat_packaging.leading: advanced packaging capex、CoWoS lead time、OSAT utilization、test hours、客户capacity reservation。
osat_packaging.lagging: packaging/test revenue、gross margin、ROIC和传统封测稼动率。
osat_packaging.margin: 先进封装mix提升毛利；传统wirebond/consumer OSAT价格竞争激烈。
osat_packaging.valuation: 看advanced packaging占比、客户承诺、capex ROI和传统业务周期底部。
osat_packaging.watchout: foundry captive packaging挤压OSAT利润池、产能扩张后利用率下滑、HBM stacking良率风险。

substrate_pcb.cycle_type: 高 beta，但AI/server PCB和consumer PCB周期差异很大；ABF扩产周期长。
substrate_pcb.drivers: layer count、ABF capacity、low-loss material、AI server/networking订单、手机/PC出货。
substrate_pcb.leading: AI PCB ASP、ABF lead time、材料mix、客户认证、server/network ODM订单。
substrate_pcb.margin: 层数和材料升级带来ASP/毛利；扩产后若需求放缓，折旧和良率爬坡会压利润。
substrate_pcb.valuation: 把AI高端板和消费板分开估值；看客户认证、良率曲线和扩产纪律。
substrate_pcb.watchout: 单位增长被ASP erosion抵消、ABF重演过剩周期、低损耗材料替代路线变化。

optical_photonics.cycle_type: 高 beta，datacom AI周期和telecom库存周期可能完全不同步。
optical_photonics.drivers: 800G/1.6T端口迁移、hyperscaler award、module ASP erosion、laser yield、CPO/silicon photonics路线。
optical_photonics.leading: customer award、module ASP、laser lead time、DSP attach、datacom/telecom mix。
optical_photonics.margin: 速率升级初期毛利较好；规模化后ASP erosion很快，需要靠良率和垂直整合抵消。
optical_photonics.valuation: 按端口迁移持续期、award质量和ASP下降速度做情景，不宜只看当年收入增速。
optical_photonics.watchout: 客户集中、模块商品化、CPO时间点误判、telecom去库存拖累headline growth。

ems_odm_systems.cycle_type: 高项目/客户 beta，收入跟订单和物料分配走，利润跟工程复杂度和现金转换走。
ems_odm_systems.drivers: AI server program、GPU allocation、rack-scale attach、ODM monthly revenue、working capital和客户集中度。
ems_odm_systems.leading: ODM月度收入、server build plan、GPU/ASIC allocation、power/cooling ready date、backlog。
ems_odm_systems.lagging: gross margin、cash conversion cycle、inventory、应收和客户项目毛利。
ems_odm_systems.margin: 硬件pass-through压低毛利率；rack integration/液冷/工程服务可提升单位利润。
ems_odm_systems.valuation: 看AI revenue质量、现金转换和客户结构；低毛利收入不能直接用高倍数年化。
ems_odm_systems.watchout: 低毛利放量、库存融资、客户砍单、单一客户治理/会计风险。

adjacent_infra.cycle_type: 中 beta，更接近数据中心/工业项目周期，而不是芯片ASP周期。
adjacent_infra.drivers: power density、liquid cooling attach、data-center backlog、fab construction、connector/cable content。
adjacent_infra.leading: power/cooling awards、utility/grid constraints、液冷采用率、fab project award、book-to-bill。
adjacent_infra.margin: 产品型部件毛利更稳；turnkey/project占比高时收入大但margin和现金流更波动。
adjacent_infra.valuation: 按backlog质量、项目毛利和AI/fab持续性估值；更适合工业capex框架。
adjacent_infra.watchout: 项目延期、turnkey稀释、客户capex double ordering、电力瓶颈导致半导体订单外溢但延后。

passives_components.cycle_type: 中-高 beta，分销库存周期明显，汽车/AI server高端mix提供结构性缓冲。
passives_components.drivers: MLCC库存、auto/industrial content、smartphone/PC units、高频高速组件和utilization。
passives_components.leading: MLCC lead time、distributor sell-through、auto production、server connector/component orders、月度收入。
passives_components.margin: 高端车规/服务器组件保护毛利；低端消费件价格竞争强，库存周期放大毛利波动。
passives_components.valuation: 看库存底部、产品mix和客户分散度；不能把所有被动件都按同一周期倍数处理。
passives_components.watchout: 补库不等于终端复苏、低端产能过剩、高端替代导致价格压力。

demand_ai_dc.drivers: 云厂商capex、GPU/ASIC/HBM供给、推理ROI、电力和机房交付。
demand_ai_dc.leading: capex revision、accelerator backlog、power/cooling order、ODM月度收入、网络端口升级。
demand_ai_dc.lagging: AI server revenue、rack deployment、utilization和客户ROI披露。
demand_ai_dc.watchout: 芯片订单很强但电力/网络/机柜交付跟不上，或客户把算力短缺当成真实需求。

demand_smartphone.drivers: 换机周期、渠道库存、新品发布、高端mix、China Android和Apple build plan。
demand_smartphone.leading: sell-through、渠道库存、面板价格、供应链拉货、运营商补贴。
demand_smartphone.lagging: chip/module revenue、EMS出货、毛利和库存跌价。
demand_smartphone.watchout: sell-in restock不等于终端需求复苏；不同元件的库存周期不同步。

demand_auto_industrial.cycle_type: Long design-in cycle + short inventory correction
demand_auto_industrial.drivers: 汽车产量、EV/SiC内容量、工业PMI、分销库存、客户项目节奏。
demand_auto_industrial.watchout: 结构性content growth可能被短期去库存完全盖住；也可能被补库存误判为长期成长。

demand_fab_localization.drivers: 政府补贴、出口限制、区域产能安全、fab建设和真实wafer demand。
demand_fab_localization.leading: fab announcement、tool order、export-control deadline、subsidy approval、construction milestone。
demand_fab_localization.watchout: 政策驱动capex可能领先真实需求太多，成熟制程本地化容易带来过剩。

demand_networking_optical.cycle_type: Port-speed migration / cloud network refresh cycle
demand_networking_optical.drivers: AI cluster topology、Ethernet vs InfiniBand、800G/1.6T迁移、customer award和module ASP。
demand_networking_optical.leading: hyperscaler award、switch roadmap、optical module ASP、laser lead time、retimer attach。
demand_networking_optical.watchout: 端口迁移会拉动出货，但模块价格下降很快；award集中度会放大单公司波动。

demand_storage_bw.cycle_type: Memory pricing cycle + structural HBM bandwidth cycle
demand_storage_bw.drivers: HBM qualification、DRAM/NAND ASP、enterprise SSD、bit supply和capex纪律。
demand_storage_bw.leading: contract price、spot price、inventory days、HBM allocation、memory capex plan。
demand_storage_bw.lagging: memory gross margin、cash flow、equipment orders和供应链capex。
demand_storage_bw.watchout: HBM强不代表commodity memory无周期；高景气下capex扩张可能埋下下行风险。

chain_edges: PDK/IP/tools turn architecture into tape-out-ready designs
Design starts and IP royalties lead future wafer demand.
Designs buy wafer capacity on specific nodes
Advanced-node scarcity decides gross margin and launch timing.
HBM allocation can be as decisive as accelerator supply.
Fab capacity and node migration drive tool orders
WFE orders are leading indicators for future wafer capacity.
Wafer starts consume wafers, gases, photoresist, CMP and chemicals
Materials lag tool orders but track utilization closely.
Higher process complexity increases metrology/inspection and test intensity
Yield bottlenecks shift value to KLA/ATE/probe ecosystems.
Finished wafers move into assembly, package and test
AI packages move the bottleneck from wafer fab to package capacity.
HBM stacking and memory test require specialized back-end capacity
HBM test/stacking complexity creates non-memory beneficiaries.
Advanced packages require ABF/BT substrates and high-end boards
Substrate shortages can cap AI accelerator shipments.
Switch/DSP/retimer decisions define port speed and optical module demand
Track architecture and port migration together.
Boards and substrates are assembled into servers, switches and racks
AI PCB content can rise faster than server unit growth.
Modules and cables are integrated into switches/racks
Optical award timing can foreshadow ODM revenue ramps.
Power devices, controllers and modules connect chips to rack/facility power
Power density moves value to cooling and power management.
Power, cooling, connectors and cable harnesses enable rack-scale systems
Data-center constraints can bottleneck semiconductor demand.
Systems are delivered to cloud, enterprise, telecom, auto and industrial customers
Demand quality must be checked beyond component pull-ins.

value_chain_scenarios.label: AI rack / data center
value_chain_scenarios.headline: AI capex is a rack-level value chain, not a single GPU line item.
value_chain_scenarios.how_to_read: Start from cloud capex and rack architecture, then follow how accelerator/HBM demand becomes wafer capacity, package/substrate bottlenecks, data movement and deployment constraints.
value_chain_scenarios.core_question: Which bottleneck is setting shipment cadence: accelerator supply, HBM, CoWoS/substrate, optical/networking, ODM integration or power/cooling?

stages.title: 1. Demand pull
stages.logic: Hyperscaler AI capex and rack architecture define the full BOM.
stages.bottleneck: Customer ROI, power availability and rack-level design freeze.
stages.signals: Cloud capex revisions; AI server backlog; utilization and inference commentary.

stages.title: 2. Compute and bandwidth
stages.logic: Accelerator/ASIC demand pulls HBM, DRAM and enterprise storage bandwidth.
stages.bottleneck: HBM qualification and accelerator allocation.
stages.signals: GPU lead times; HBM contract pricing; memory capex discipline.

stages.title: 3. Wafer and process stack
stages.logic: Design starts become advanced-node wafers; process complexity raises WFE/material/test intensity.
stages.bottleneck: Advanced-node capacity, yield learning and export-control constraints.
stages.signals: Foundry HPC mix; WFE orders/backlog; process-control intensity.

stages.title: 4. Package and interconnect
stages.logic: Known-good dies become advanced packages, ABF substrates, high-layer PCB and component-heavy boards.
stages.bottleneck: CoWoS/2.5D capacity, substrate lead time and low-loss material yield.
stages.signals: Advanced packaging capex; substrate lead time; AI PCB ASP/layer count.

stages.title: 5. Data movement
stages.logic: Scale-out clusters need switch silicon, retimers, optical DSP, modules and lasers.
stages.bottleneck: Port-speed migration, optical yield and customer award concentration.
stages.signals: 800G/1.6T awards; Ethernet vs InfiniBand share; optical module ASP erosion.

stages.title: 6. System delivery
stages.logic: Components become AI servers, racks and facilities with power/cooling constraints.
stages.bottleneck: Rack integration, working capital, power density and liquid cooling availability.
stages.signals: ODM monthly revenue; AI rack backlog; power/cooling order books.

value_chain_scenarios.label: Smartphone / consumer
value_chain_scenarios.headline: The handset chain is a unit-cycle plus content-cycle map.
value_chain_scenarios.how_to_read: Start from handset units and premium mix, separate SoC/RF/CIS/DDI/memory content, then check foundry utilization, PCB/HDI, passives and EMS build plans.
value_chain_scenarios.core_question: Is the revenue recovery unit-driven restocking or real content growth?

stages.title: 1. Demand pull
stages.logic: Handset units, premium mix and channel inventory set the cycle.
stages.bottleneck: Sell-through quality after restock.
stages.signals: China Android sell-through; Apple build plans; channel inventory.

stages.title: 2. Device silicon
stages.logic: SoC, modem, RF, connectivity, display driver, memory and PMIC content move with model mix.
stages.bottleneck: Apple insourcing, Android competition and RF ASP pressure.
stages.signals: Design wins; premium mix; RF front-end content per device.

stages.title: 3. Wafer and materials
stages.logic: Mobile chips use advanced logic plus mature/specialty processes and test capacity.
stages.bottleneck: Utilization and mature-node pricing.
stages.signals: Foundry utilization; specialty process bookings; tester demand.

stages.title: 4. Board and components
stages.logic: HDI, passives, sensors and connectors convert model mix into hardware BOM.
stages.bottleneck: Inventory correction and component pricing.
stages.signals: PCB monthly revenue; MLCC inventory; sensor/module orders.

stages.title: 5. Assembly
stages.logic: EMS/ODM build plans validate whether upstream orders are sell-through or pull-in.
stages.bottleneck: Build-plan cuts and channel digestion.
stages.signals: EMS monthly revenue; OEM build revisions; working capital.

value_chain_scenarios.label: Auto / industrial
value_chain_scenarios.headline: Auto/industrial is a long design-in chain with a short inventory-cycle overlay.
value_chain_scenarios.how_to_read: Start from auto production, EV inverter demand and industrial PMI; separate long-cycle content growth from distributor inventory correction.
value_chain_scenarios.core_question: Are earnings revisions driven by secular content gains or by inventory/utilization swings?

stages.title: 1. Demand pull
stages.logic: Auto production, EV platforms and industrial capex create long-cycle design-in demand.
stages.bottleneck: EV slowdown and industrial inventory digestion.
stages.signals: Auto production; industrial PMI; distributor inventory.

stages.title: 2. Control and power silicon
stages.logic: MCU, analog, power, sensors, SiC/GaN and passives are tied to design-in quality.
stages.bottleneck: SiC price pressure and China analog competition.
stages.signals: Book-to-bill; SiC utilization; backlog quality.

stages.title: 3. Specialty manufacturing
stages.logic: BCD, power, SiC/GaN and mature-node capacity determine supply and margin.
stages.bottleneck: Utilization normalization and mature-node overcapacity.
stages.signals: Specialty foundry utilization; wafer shipments; test intensity.

stages.title: 4. System integration
stages.logic: Industrial EMS, power equipment and connectors convert semis into end systems.
stages.bottleneck: Project timing and channel restocking quality.
stages.signals: Industrial backlog; connector orders; project margins.

value_chain_scenarios.label: Fab buildout / localization
value_chain_scenarios.headline: Fab localization turns policy and capex into tools, materials and yield-control demand.
value_chain_scenarios.how_to_read: Start with announced fab plans and export controls, then separate real utilization demand from capex pull-forward.
value_chain_scenarios.core_question: Is demand structural localization or temporary pull-forward ahead of restrictions?

stages.title: 1. Demand pull
stages.logic: Sovereignty, subsidies and capacity plans start the fab-buildout cycle.
stages.bottleneck: Export controls and subsidy-led overcapacity.
stages.signals: Government incentives; fab announcements; tool restriction changes.

stages.title: 2. Tool stack
stages.logic: Lithography, etch, deposition, clean, inspection, metrology and ATE build capacity and protect yield.
stages.bottleneck: Critical-tool availability and domestic substitution quality.
stages.signals: WFE orders/backlog; China mix; inspection intensity.

stages.title: 3. Consumables
stages.logic: Wafers, gases, photoresist and CMP materials convert installed tools into wafer starts.
stages.bottleneck: Qualification and advanced-node material readiness.
stages.signals: Wafer shipments; gas/material contracts; qualification wins.

stages.title: 4. Wafer output
stages.logic: Foundry/memory utilization determines whether capex becomes profitable revenue.
stages.bottleneck: Mature-node overcapacity and memory capex discipline.
stages.signals: Utilization; wafer ASP; memory capex plans.

stages.title: 5. Back-end pull
stages.logic: New wafer output eventually pulls package, test and substrate demand.
stages.bottleneck: Advanced packaging qualification and substrate supply.
stages.signals: OSAT utilization; advanced package capex; ABF/BT lead time.

value_chain_scenarios.label: Networking / optical
value_chain_scenarios.headline: Data movement links silicon architecture to optical modules and networking systems.
value_chain_scenarios.how_to_read: Start from cluster topology and port-speed migration; then connect switch silicon, retimers/DSP, modules, boards and systems.
value_chain_scenarios.core_question: Is port-speed migration creating durable value or just pulling forward module demand?

stages.title: 1. Demand pull
stages.logic: Cloud network refresh and AI cluster topology define bandwidth demand.
stages.bottleneck: Architecture timing and customer concentration.
stages.signals: Ethernet vs InfiniBand share; 800G/1.6T awards; cloud network capex.

stages.title: 2. Silicon architecture
stages.logic: Switch ASIC, DSP, retimer, SerDes IP and foundry capacity set the electrical layer.
stages.bottleneck: SerDes performance and advanced-node availability.
stages.signals: Switch roadmap; optical DSP share; custom silicon tape-outs.

stages.title: 3. Optical conversion
stages.logic: Lasers, modules, coherent optics and silicon photonics translate speed into shipments.
stages.bottleneck: Laser yield, ASP erosion and CPO timing.
stages.signals: Module ASP; laser lead time; datacom vs telecom mix.

stages.title: 4. Board and system
stages.logic: Low-loss boards, connectors and ODM systems validate real deployment.
stages.bottleneck: Board materials and system build timing.
stages.signals: Network ODM revenue; PCB material mix; customer award cadence.

value_chain_scenarios.label: Memory / storage bandwidth
value_chain_scenarios.headline: Memory is both a structural bandwidth bottleneck and a cyclical pricing pool.
value_chain_scenarios.how_to_read: Separate HBM structural mix from commodity DRAM/NAND cycle, then track WFE/test/package bottlenecks and storage demand.
value_chain_scenarios.core_question: Is margin expansion coming from durable HBM mix or from peak-cycle memory pricing?

stages.title: 1. Demand pull
stages.logic: AI bandwidth, enterprise SSD and nearline storage create bit and mix demand.
stages.bottleneck: Hidden inventory and capex overreaction.
stages.signals: HBM qualification; DRAM/NAND contract ASP; enterprise SSD demand.

stages.title: 2. Memory supply
stages.logic: DRAM, NAND and HBM profitability depends on ASP, bit growth and capex discipline.
stages.bottleneck: Capex overshoot and qualification share loss.
stages.signals: Inventory days; capex plans; HBM share.

stages.title: 3. Fab stack
stages.logic: Memory transitions pull deposition/etch, wafers/materials, probe and ATE intensity.
stages.bottleneck: HBM test time and node transition costs.
stages.signals: Memory WFE orders; tester backlog; probe-card ASP.

stages.title: 4. Package and systems
stages.logic: HBM stack, package substrate and server/storage systems convert bit demand into deployed capacity.
stages.bottleneck: HBM stacking yield and substrate availability.
stages.signals: Advanced package capacity; substrate lead time; storage system orders.

value_chain_columns.title: Demand pull
value_chain_columns.subtitle: 终端需求先决定系统架构和BOM

value_chain_columns.title: Design / architecture
value_chain_columns.subtitle: 系统需求转化为芯片规格

value_chain_columns.title: Wafer manufacturing
value_chain_columns.subtitle: 制造能力、良率和材料约束

demand_ai_dc.role: Hyperscaler capex, accelerator deployment, rack-scale architecture, inference utilization, power availability.
demand_ai_dc.products: Compute/AI chips, HBM, foundry, advanced packaging, substrate/PCB, networking silicon, optical, EMS/ODM, power/cooling, connectors.
demand_ai_dc.questions: Do not stop at GPU revenue. Build rack-level value bridge: accelerator + HBM + package/substrate + switch/optics + power/cooling + ODM margin.
demand_ai_dc.risk: Customer ROI fatigue, power bottlenecks, double ordering, custom ASIC substitution, supply bottleneck migration away from semis.
demand_ai_dc.core_names: NVIDIA, Broadcom, AMD, Marvell, TSMC, SK hynix, Micron, ASE, Amkor, Unimicron, Ibiden, Innolight, Eoptolink, Arista, Quanta, Wiwynn, Vertiv, Delta, Amphenol.

demand_smartphone.role: Handset units, premium mix, China Android sell-through, Apple build plans, panel pricing, channel inventory.
demand_smartphone.products: Mobile SoC/modem, RF front-end, memory, display driver, image sensor, passives, EMS/ODM, PCB/HDI.
demand_smartphone.questions: Separate unit recovery from content growth. RF/CIS/SoC/DDI all have different ASP and inventory behavior even when tied to the same handset cycle.
demand_smartphone.risk: Weak sell-through after channel restock, Apple insourcing, Android price competition, display driver commoditization.
demand_smartphone.core_names: Qualcomm, MediaTek, Skyworks, Qorvo, Maxscend, Will Semi, Sony, GalaxyCore, Novatek, Samsung Electronics, TSMC, Murata, Yageo, Hon Hai.

demand_auto_industrial.role: Auto production, EV inverter demand, industrial PMI, distributor inventory, long-cycle design-in conversion.
demand_auto_industrial.products: MCU, analog, power, sensors, SiC/GaN, passives, specialty foundry, test, industrial EMS.
demand_auto_industrial.questions: Use a longer-cycle framework: design-in wins and content per vehicle matter, but near-term earnings are still dominated by inventory and utilization.
demand_auto_industrial.risk: EV slowdown, SiC price pressure, customer inventory correction, China analog/power competition.
demand_auto_industrial.core_names: NXP, Infineon, STMicro, Renesas, ON Semi, TI, ADI, ROHM, StarPower, Melexis, X-FAB, Tower, Murata, TDK, Yageo.

demand_fab_localization.role: TSMC/Samsung/Intel/SMIC/Micron capex, government incentives, export controls, regional capacity plans.
demand_fab_localization.products: WFE, process control, materials, wafers, specialty gases, fab services, vacuum/precision components.
demand_fab_localization.questions: Separate technology intensity from capacity additions. Advanced nodes raise equipment/material content; mature-node localization can still create overcapacity.
demand_fab_localization.risk: Export controls, China pull-forward, capex pauses, subsidy-led returns below cost of capital.
demand_fab_localization.core_names: ASML, Applied Materials, Lam, KLA, Tokyo Electron, ASM International, Advantest, Screen, DISCO, Shin-Etsu, SUMCO, Entegris, Linde, Air Liquide, NAURA, AMEC.

demand_networking_optical.role: Port-speed migration, AI cluster topology, Ethernet vs InfiniBand, 800G/1.6T awards, cloud network refresh.
demand_networking_optical.products: Switch ASIC, optical DSP, retimers, active cables, optical modules, lasers, PCB/low-loss materials, networking systems.
demand_networking_optical.questions: Build a data-movement chain from switch silicon to module to cable to system. ASP erosion and speed migration must be modeled together.
demand_networking_optical.risk: Rapid module commoditization, customer concentration, CPO timing, port-speed pull-forward.
demand_networking_optical.core_names: Broadcom, Marvell, NVIDIA, Credo, Astera Labs, MACOM, Coherent, Lumentum, Fabrinet, Ciena, Innolight, Eoptolink, Arista, Accton.

demand_storage_bw.role: HBM qualification, DRAM/NAND contract prices, enterprise SSD demand, nearline HDD demand, memory capex discipline.
demand_storage_bw.products: DRAM, HBM, NAND, storage controllers, enterprise SSD, HDD, OSAT/test, WFE, wafers/materials.
demand_storage_bw.questions: Treat HBM as structural mix and commodity DRAM/NAND as cycle. Use normalized earnings and do not annualize peak pricing blindly.
demand_storage_bw.risk: Capex overreaction, HBM share loss, NAND overbuild, hidden channel inventory.
demand_storage_bw.core_names: SK hynix, Micron, Samsung Electronics, Kioxia, Sandisk, Western Digital, Seagate, Phison, Silicon Motion, Advantest, Lam, Applied Materials.

region_lenses.strengths: AI accelerators, EDA/IP, analog, WFE/process control, networking silicon, storage systems and data-center infrastructure.
region_lenses.weaknesses: Advanced manufacturing share, some packaging/substrate dependence, Asia-heavy supply chain for many hardware layers.
region_lenses.companies: NVIDIA, Broadcom, AMD, Qualcomm, Marvell, Synopsys, Cadence, Applied Materials, Lam, KLA, TI, ADI, Micron, Amkor, Vertiv, Amphenol.
region_lenses.research_angle: US coverage often starts with design/platform economics, then checks supply bottlenecks in Taiwan/Korea/Japan and downstream AI capex.

region_lenses.strengths: Foundry, ASIC services, fabless SoC, OSAT, substrates/PCB, server ODM, optical modules and AI server supply chain.
region_lenses.weaknesses: Geopolitical concentration, power/water constraints, dependence on imported equipment/materials at the highest end.
region_lenses.companies: TSMC, UMC, MediaTek, GUC, Alchip, ASE, Unimicron, Elite Material, Quanta, Wiwynn, Hon Hai, GlobalWafers, Realtek, Novatek.
region_lenses.research_angle: Taiwan is the physical center of AI hardware conversion from design to rack. Monthly revenue data can be a powerful high-frequency signal.

region_lenses.strengths: Memory/HBM, NAND, advanced packaging equipment, materials, test consumables, substrate/PCB and Samsung-led ecosystem.
region_lenses.weaknesses: Memory cyclicality, customer concentration in domestic giants, foundry share uncertainty.
region_lenses.companies: Samsung Electronics, SK hynix, Samsung Electro-Mechanics, Hanmi Semiconductor, HPSP, Wonik IPS, Soulbrain, ISC, Leeno, Simmtech.
region_lenses.research_angle: Korea is the highest-signal region for HBM, memory capex and memory-linked back-end/test bottlenecks.

region_lenses.strengths: WFE, metrology, precision processing, wafers, photoresist/materials, photomasks, components and specialty semis.
region_lenses.weaknesses: Fewer leading-edge logic designers; some companies are conglomerates where semi exposure must be isolated.
region_lenses.companies: Tokyo Electron, Advantest, DISCO, Screen, Lasertec, Shin-Etsu, SUMCO, Tokyo Ohka, Renesas, Sony, Ibiden, Murata, TDK.
region_lenses.research_angle: Japan is the cleanest lens for manufacturing intensity: process complexity, materials content and precision equipment.

region_lenses.strengths: Mature foundry, domestic fabless, RF/analog/power localization, WFE/materials substitution, OSAT, PCB, optical modules and EMS.
region_lenses.weaknesses: Advanced-node equipment restrictions, technology gaps in EUV/EDA/high-end materials, policy-driven overcapacity risk.
region_lenses.companies: SMIC, Hua Hong, Will Semi, Maxscend, SG Micro, GigaDevice, Montage, Hygon, Cambricon, NAURA, AMEC, Piotech, JCET, Tongfu, Innolight.
region_lenses.research_angle: China coverage should separate real share gain from subsidy/capex cycles; export-control headlines can change both fundamentals and multiples.

region_lenses.strengths: Lithography, deposition, packaging equipment, power/auto semis, specialty foundry, engineered substrates, industrial power/cooling.
region_lenses.weaknesses: Limited advanced-node fabless ecosystem and lower exposure to direct AI accelerator design.
region_lenses.companies: ASML, ASM International, BE Semiconductor, Infineon, STMicro, X-FAB, Soitec, Siltronic, VAT, Schneider Electric, Melexis.
region_lenses.research_angle: Europe is a strong auto/industrial and manufacturing-enablement region; valuation often hinges on cycle normalization and strategic scarcity.

region_lenses.strengths: OSAT, test, EMS, precision manufacturing, optical manufacturing and supply-chain diversification.
region_lenses.weaknesses: Fewer global design leaders; many names are services/manufacturing with customer concentration.
region_lenses.companies: Fabrinet, Inari Amertron, Unisem, MPI, AEM, UMS, Venture, Frencken, Vitrox, Greatech.
region_lenses.research_angle: ASEAN is the diversification and back-end manufacturing lens: follow utilization, customer wins and relocation-driven capex.

official_market_context.latest_public_read: April 2026 global semiconductor sales were $110.5B, up 11.0% month-to-month and 93.9% year-to-year.
official_market_context.source: SIA/WSTS April 2026 global semiconductor sales release
official_market_context.as_of: Release date 2026-06-05; monthly sales are a three-month moving average.
official_market_context.investment_use: Use as a top-down cycle gauge and demand-momentum check, not as a company-level revenue proxy.
official_market_context.coverage_implication: Elevates AI-exposed compute, memory, networking, foundry, OSAT, substrate/PCB, optical and power/cooling chains.

official_market_context.latest_public_read: SIA endorsed WSTS Spring 2026 forecast projecting 2026 global semiconductor sales growth of 90% to $1.5T and 2027 sales above $1.9T.
official_market_context.investment_use: Treat as top-down market context; pressure-test against company guidance, channel inventory and pricing.
official_market_context.coverage_implication: Requires separate bull/base/bear logic for AI winners versus lagging auto/industrial/consumer chains.

official_market_context.latest_public_read: SIA/Deloitte reported chips account for more than 95% of a leading AI server rack content value and over 50% of AI data-center capex.
official_market_context.source: SIA/Deloitte AI data-center stack study release
official_market_context.investment_use: Use to justify rack-level value bridge rather than stopping at accelerator revenue.
official_market_context.coverage_implication: For AI coverage, model accelerator + HBM + package/substrate + networking/optical + power/analog + system integration.

official_market_context.latest_public_read: The SIA/Deloitte release states annual revenue from semiconductors used in AI data centers could reach over $1.2T by 2028.
official_market_context.source: SIA/Deloitte AI data-center stack study release
official_market_context.investment_use: Use as a strategic TAM marker, then reconcile to company-specific capacity, share and ASP assumptions.
official_market_context.coverage_implication: High TAM does not remove bottleneck risk; monitor power, HBM, CoWoS, optical and customer ROI.

official_market_context.topic: AI rack chip count and full-stack demand
official_market_context.latest_public_read: The SIA/Deloitte release says a single AI server rack contains over 4,500 packaged chips across logic, memory, analog and foundational technologies.
official_market_context.source: SIA/Deloitte AI data-center stack study release
official_market_context.investment_use: Use to map second- and third-order beneficiaries beyond GPU/ASIC leaders.
official_market_context.coverage_implication: Supports including analog/power, sensors/controllers, passives, interconnect and EMS/ODM in the semi-tech mapping boundary.

official_market_context.latest_public_read: SEMI reported 2025 silicon wafer shipments increased 5.8% to 12,973 MSI while revenue slipped 1.2% to $11.4B.
official_market_context.source: SEMI Silicon Manufacturers Group 2025 year-end release
official_market_context.investment_use: Use as materials-layer cycle check: units can recover while pricing/mix revenue remains soft.
official_market_context.coverage_implication: Separate advanced logic/HBM wafer demand from mature-node recovery and legacy pricing pressure.

data_governance_layers.examples: SIA/WSTS sales releases, SEMI wafer/equipment/materials releases, official industry reports.
data_governance_layers.allowed_use: Market size, cycle direction, source methodology, high-level end-market framing.
data_governance_layers.not_allowed_use: Do not infer company revenue, margins, valuation or market share without company/CIQ tie-out.

data_governance_layers.examples: Annual reports, 10-K/20-F, exchange filings, investor presentations, earnings calls and official IR tables.
data_governance_layers.allowed_use: Company description, segment exposure, customer concentration, products, capex, financial history.
data_governance_layers.not_allowed_use: Do not mix fiscal years, currencies or dual listings without explicit normalization.

data_governance_layers.examples: CIQ export: market cap, EV, FY revenue, GM, EV/Sales, P/E, consensus growth, ownership and short interest.
data_governance_layers.allowed_use: Valuation screens, comp tables, liquidity/ownership checks and estimate-revision monitoring.
data_governance_layers.not_allowed_use: Do not publish stale values or combine non-synchronous prices, shares and estimates.

data_governance_layers.examples: Priority tier, segment mapping, bull/base/bear variables, key debates, coverage waves and research actions.
data_governance_layers.allowed_use: Investment framing, research workflow, monitoring design and thesis/falsifier construction.
data_governance_layers.not_allowed_use: Do not present judgment as sourced fact; keep it separate from official data and CIQ fields.
